element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Congo reverts to monkey form on first hit; death on second hit
Natural/organic setting with vegetation and terrain hazards
Nautical setting with unique spatial constraints
Supernatural/atmospheric challenges leading to final confrontation
Progressive boss lineup: demon, T-Rex, ninja, pirate, mad scientist, vampire

typical_tension_triggers: Two-hit death mechanic (human form to monkey form to death)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Moderate pacing with varied level difficulty; reviewed as 'too easy' by some critics but containing sufficient variety to maintain engagement across 35 levels

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory platforming with gentle enemy introduction
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating environmental hazards and enemy complexity across varied biomes (mountain, pirate ship)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Peak difficulty with volcano and ghost town environments combined with advanced boss encounters
notable_spikes: Volcano world (World 4) - introduction of environmental hazards
Ghost town (World 5) - atmospheric and mechanical difficulty peak
Final boss encounters - progressive boss difficulty escalation

notable_level_patterns.description: 35 levels organized across 5 distinct worlds (jungle, mountain, pirate ship, volcano, ghost town), each with unique environmental and aesthetic characteristics that introduce different mechanical challenges
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Volcano world introduces lava hazards; ghost town introduces supernatural elements

notable_level_patterns.description: Each world culminates in a boss encounter serving as progression gate. Boss roster includes demon, T-Rex, ninja, pirate, mad scientist, and vampire, creating escalating challenge variety
Congo's health reverts from human form to monkey form on first enemy hit; second hit results in death. This creates distinctive tension rhythm throughout all levels

sources_used: Wikipedia - Congo's Caper (gameplay section, level/world count, boss roster, difficulty notes from reviews)
The Cutting Room Floor - Congo's Caper (confirmation of SNES platform, Data East developer, level select feature)
